author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-14. Saint Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of St. John and the Epistle to the Hebrews

source.title: NPNF1-14. Saint Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of St. John and the Epistle to the Hebrews

title: John 7.37,38

source.title: NPNF1-14. Saint Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of St. John and the Epistle to the Hebrews

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “In the last day, the great day of the feast, Jesus stood and cried, saying, If any man thirst, let him come unto Me, and drink.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He that believeth on Me, as the Scripture hath said, out of his belly shall flow rivers of living water.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They who come to the divine preaching and give heed to the faith, must manifest the desire of thirsty men for water, and kindle in themselves a similar longing; so will they be able also very carefully to retain what is said.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For as thirsty men, when they have taken a bowl, eagerly drain it and then desist, so too they who hear the divine oracles if they receive them thirsting, will never be weary until they have drunk them up.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For to show that men ought ever to thirst and hunger, “Blessed,” It saith, “are they which do hunger and thirst after righteousness” ( Matt. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 6 ); and here Christ saith, “If any man thirst, let him come unto Me, and drink.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What He saith is of this kind, “I draw no man to Me by necessity and constraint; but if any hath great zeal, if any is inflamed with desire, him I call.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But why hath the Evangelist remarked that it was “on the last day, that great day”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For both the first day and the last were “great,” while the intermediate days they spent rather in enjoyment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore then saith he, “in the last day”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because on that day they were all collected together.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For on the first day He came not, and told the reason to His brethren, nor yet on the second and third days saith He anything of this kind, lest His words should come to nought, the hearers being about to run into indulgence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But on the last day when they were returning home He giveth them supplies for their salvation, and crieth aloud, partly by this showing to us His boldness, and partly for the greatness of the multitude.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And to show that He spake not of material drink, He addeth, “He that believeth on Me, as the Scripture hath said, out of his belly shall flow rivers of living water.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By “belly” he here meaneth the heart, as also in another place It saith, “And Thy Law in the midst of my belly.” ( Ps. xl.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 10 ; Theodotion.)

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But where hath the Scripture said, that “rivers of living water shall flow from his belly”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then meaneth, “He that believeth on Me, as the Scripture saith”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Here we must place a stop, so that the, “rivers shall flow from his belly,” may be an assertion of Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For because many said, “This is the Christ”; and, “When the Christ cometh will He do more miracles?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He showeth that it behooveth to have a correct knowledge, and to be convinced not so much from the miracles as from the Scriptures.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Many, in fact, who even saw Him working marvels received Him not as Christ, and were ready to say, “Do not the Scriptures say that Christ cometh of the seed of David?” and on this they continually dwelt.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He then, desiring to show that He did not shun the proof from the Scriptures, again referreth them to the Scriptures.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He had said before, “Search the Scriptures” ( c. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 39 ); and again, “It is written in the Prophets, And they shall be taught of God” ( c. vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 45 ); and, “Moses accuseth you” ( c. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 45 ); and here,“As the Scripture hath said, rivers shall flow from his belly,” alluding to the largeness and abundance of grace.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As in another place He saith, “A well of water springing up unto eternal life” ( c. iv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 14 ), that is to say, “he shall possess much grace”; and elsewhere He calleth it, “eternal life,” but here, “living water.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He calleth that “living” which ever worketh; for the grace of the Spirit, when it hath entered into the mind and hath been established, springeth up more than any fountain, faileth not, becometh not empty, stayeth not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To signify therefore at once its unfailing supply and unlimited operation, He hath called it “a well” and “rivers,” not one river but numberless; and in the former case He hath represented its abundance by the expression, “springing.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And one may clearly perceive what is meant, if he will consider the wisdom of Stephen, the tongue of Peter, the vehemence of Paul, how nothing bare, nothing withstood them, not the anger of multitudes, not the risings up of tyrants, not the plots of devils, not daily deaths, but as rivers borne along with a great rushing sound, so they went on their way hurrying all things with them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But this spake He of the Spirit, which they that believe on Him should receive; for the Holy Ghost was not yet given.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How then did the Prophets prophesy and work those ten thousand wonders?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the Apostles cast not out devils by the Spirit, but by power received from Him; as He saith Himself, “If I by Beelzebub cast out devils, by whom do your children cast them out?” ( Matt. xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this He said, signifying that before the Crucifixion not all cast out devils by the Spirit, but that some did so by the power received from Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So when He was about to send them, He said, “Receive ye the Holy Ghost” ( c. xx.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 22 ); and again, “The Holy Ghost came upon them” ( Acts xix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 6 ), and then they wrought miracles.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But when He was sending them, the Scripture said not, that “He gave to them the Holy Ghost,” but that He gave to them “power,” saying, “Cleanse the lepers, cast out devils, raise the dead, freely ye have received, freely give.” ( Matt. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But in the case of the Prophets, all allow that the Gift was that of the Holy Spirit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But this Grace was stinted and departed and failed from off the earth, from the day in which it was said, “Your house is left unto you desolate” ( Matt. xxiii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 38 ); and even before that day its dearth had begun, for there was no longer any prophet among them, nor did Grace visit their holy things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since then the Holy Ghost had been withheld, but was for the future to be shed forth abundantly, and since the beginning of this imparting was after the Crucifixion, not only as to its abundance, but also as to the increased greatness of the gifts, (for the Gift was more marvelous, as when It saith, “Ye know not what Spirit ye are of” ( Luke ix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 55 ); and again, “For ye have not received the Spirit of bondage, but the Spirit of adoption” ( Rom. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 15 ); and the men of old possessed the Spirit themselves, but imparted It not to others, while the Apostles filled tens of thousands with It,) since then, I say, they were to receive this Gift, but It was not yet given, for this cause he addeth, “The Holy Ghost was not yet.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since then the Lord spoke of this grace, the Evangelist hath said, “For the Holy Ghost was not yet,” that is, “was not yet given,”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Because Jesus was not yet glorified.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Calling the Cross, “glory.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since we were enemies, and had sinned, and fallen short of the gift of God, and were haters of God, and since grace was a proof of our reconciliation, and since a gift is not given to those who are hated, but to friends and those who have been well-pleasing; it was therefore necessary that the Sacrifice should first be offered for us, that the enmity (against God) which was in our flesh should be done away, that we should become friends of God, and so receive the Gift.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if this was done with respect to the promise made to Abraham, much more with respect to grace.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this Paul hath declared, saying, “If they which are of the Law be heirs, faith is made void—because the Law worketh wrath.” ( Rom. iv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What he saith, is of this kind: God “promised that He would give the earth to Abraham and to his seed: but his descendants were unworthy of the promise, and of their own deeds could not be well-pleasing unto God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On this account came in faith, an easy action, that it might draw grace unto it, and that the promise might not fail.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And It saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Therefore it is of faith, that it might be by grace, to the end the promise might be sure.” ( Rom. iv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore it is by grace, since by their own labors they prevailed not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But wherefore after saying, “according to the Scriptures,” did He not add the testimony?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because their mind was corrupt; for,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Some said, This is the Prophet.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Others said, He deceiveth the people; others said, Christ cometh not from Galilee, but from the village of Bethlehem.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Others said, “When Christ cometh, no man knoweth whence He is” ( ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 27 ); and there was a difference of opinion, as might be expected in a confused multitude; for not attentively did they listen to His words, nor for the sake of learning.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore He maketh them no answer; yet they said, “Doth Christ come out of Galilee?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And He had praised, as being “an Israelite indeed,” Nathanael, who had said in a more forcible and striking manner, “Can there any good thing come out of Nazareth?” ( c. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But then these men, and they who said to Nicodemus, “Search and look, for out of Galilee ariseth no prophet” ( ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 52 ), said it not seeking to learn, but merely to overturn the opinion concerning Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Nathanael said this, being a lover of the truth, and knowing exactly all the ancient histories; but they looked only to one thing, and that was to remove the opinion that He was the Christ, on which account He revealed nothing to them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For they who even contradicted themselves, and said at one time, “No man knoweth whence He cometh,” at another, “From Bethlehem,” would manifestly even if they had been informed have opposed Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For be it that they knew not the place of His birth, that He was from Bethlehem, because of His dwelling in Nazareth, (yet this cannot be allowed, for He was not born there,) were they ignorant of His race also, that He was “of the house and lineage of David”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How then said they, “Doth not Christ come of the seed of David?” ( Ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because they wished to conceal even this fact by that question, saying all that they said with malicious intent.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why did they not come to Him and say, “Since we admire thee in other respects, and thou biddest us believe thee according to the Scriptures, tell us how it is that the Scriptures say that Christ must come from Bethlehem, when thou art come from Galilee?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But they said nothing of the kind, but all in malice.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And to show that they spoke not enquiringly, nor as desiring to learn, the Evangelist straightway hath added, that,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Some of them would have taken Him, but no man laid his hand upon Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This, if nothing else, might have been sufficient to cause compunction in them, but they felt it not, as the Prophet saith, “They were cleft asunder, and were not pricked in heart.” ( Ps. xxxv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Such a thing is malice! it will give way to nothing, it looks to one thing only, and that is, to destroy the person against whom it plotteth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But what saith the Scripture?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Whoso diggeth a pit for his neighbor, shall fall into it himself.” ( Prov. xxvi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Which was the case then.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For they desired to kill Him, to stop, as they thought, His preaching; the result was the opposite.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the preaching flourishes by the grace of Christ, while all that was theirs is quenched and perished; they have lost their country, their freedom, their security, their worship, they have been deprived of all their prosperity, and are become slaves and captives.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Knowing then this, let us never plot against others, aware that by so doing we whet the sword against ourselves, and inflict upon ourselves the deeper wound.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Hath any one grieved thee, and desireth thou to avenge thyself on him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Avenge not thyself; so shalt thou be able to be avenged; but if thou avenge thyself, thou art not avenged.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Think not that this is a riddle, but a true saying.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “How, and in what way?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because if thou avenge not thyself on him, thou makest God his enemy; but if thou avenge thyself, no longer so.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Vengeance is Mine, I will repay, saith the Lord.” ( Rom. xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if we have servants, and they having quarreled with each other, do not give place to us for judgment and for punishment, but take it upon themselves; though they come to us ten thousand times, we not only shall not avenge them, but shall even be wroth with them, saying, “Thou runaway, thou flogging-post, thou oughtest to have submitted all to us, but since thou hast prevented us and avenged thyself, trouble us no farther”; much more shall God, who hath bidden us commit all unto Him, say this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For how can it be otherwise than absurd, when we demand from our servants so much minding of wisdom and obedience, but will not yield to our Master in those matters in which we desire our domestics to yield to us?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This I say because of your readiness to inflict punishment one upon another.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The truly wise man ought not to do this even, but to pardon and forgive offenses, though there were not that great reward proposed, the receiving in return forgiveness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For, tell me, if thou condemnest one who hath sinned, wherefore dost thou sin thyself, and fall into the same fault?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Hath he insulted?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Insult not thou again, or thou hast insulted thyself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Hath he struck?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Strike not thou again, for then there is no difference between you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Hath he vexed thee?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Vex him not again, for the profit is nothing, and thou wilt in thy turn be placed on an equality with those who have wronged thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thus, if thou bear with meekness and gentleness, thou shalt be able to reprove thine enemy, to shame him, to weary him of being wroth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: No man cures evil with evil, but evil with good.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: These rules of wisdom give some of the heathen; now if there be such wisdom among the foolish heathen, let us be ashamed to show ourselves inferior to them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Many of them have been injured, and have borne it; many have been maliciously accused, and not defended themselves; have been plotted against, and have repaid by benefits.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And there is no small fear lest some of them be found in their lives to be greater than we, and so render our punishment severer.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when we who have partaken of the Spirit, we who look for the Kingdom, who follow wisdom for the sake of heavenly things, who fear (not) hell, and are bidden to become angels, who enjoy the Mysteries; when we reach not to the virtue unto which they have attained, what pardon shall we have?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If we must go beyond the Jews, (for, “Except your righteousness shall exceed the righteousness of the Scribes and Pharisees, ye shall in no case enter into the Kingdom of Heaven”— Matt. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 20 ,) much more the heathen; if the Pharisees, much more the unbelievers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since if when we go not beyond the righteousness of the Jews, the Kingdom is shut against us, how shall we be able to attain unto it when we prove ourselves worse than the heathen?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then cast out all bitterness, and wrath, and anger.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To speak “the same things, to me indeed is not grievous, but for you it is safe,” ( Philip. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For physicians also often use the same remedy, and we will not cease from sounding the same things in your ears, reminding, teaching, exhorting, for great is the tumult of worldly things, and it causes in us forgetfulness, and we have need of continual teaching.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then, in order that we meet not together in this place uselessly and in vain, exhibit the proof which is by works, that so we may obtain the good things to come, through the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, by whom and with whom, to the Father and the Holy Ghost be glory, now and ever and world without end.
